Team Lead, Digital Channels Job at Union Bank Plc




        
    

        
        Union Bank of Nigeria ("UBN") was established in 1917 and is one of Nigeria’s long-standing and most respected financial institutions, offering a portfolio of banking services to individuals, SMEs, commercial and corporate clients. With a robust geographical network comprising more than 300 service centres and over 950+ ATMs spread across Nigeria, we have remained committed to helping individuals, families and businesses grow for nearly a century.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Team Lead, Digital Channels

Job Location: Lagos

Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Interested applicant(s) should possess relevant qualifications and experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Receptionist",
  "description": "HRLeverage Africa Consulting Limited&nbsp;- Our client is&nbsp;a growing company in the travel and visa services industry, providing professional support to individuals seeking international opportunities.&nbsp;

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Receptionist will manage front desk operations and serve as the face of the organization, ensuring a welcoming and professional environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to visitors and clients.
	Answer phone calls and direct inquiries appropriately.
	Manage appointment scheduling and office coordination.
	Maintain a clean and organized reception area.
	Handle basic administrative duties.
	Support internal teams with clerical tasks.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND qualification.
	Proven experience in a front desk or administrative role.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Good appearance and professional demeanor.
	Basic computer skills.


Salary
N150,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr.hrleverageafrica@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Receptionist &ndash; Lagos&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Receptionist Job at HRLeverage Africa Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        HRLeverage Africa Consulting Limited - Our client is a growing company in the travel and visa services industry, providing professional support to individuals seeking international opportunities. 

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Receptionist will manage front desk operations and serve as the face of the organization, ensuring a welcoming and professional environment.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Welcome and attend to visitors and clients.
	Answer phone calls and direct inquiries appropriately.
	Manage appointment scheduling and office coordination.
	Maintain a clean and organized reception area.
	Handle basic administrative duties.
	Support internal teams with clerical tasks.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND qualification.
	Proven experience in a front desk or administrative role.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Good appearance and professional demeanor.
	Basic computer skills.


Salary
N150,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Receptionist – Lagos" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Control Accountant",
  "description": "Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company&#39;s flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Inventory Control Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Apapa, Lagos

Purpose


	To effectively monitor all inventory transactions relating to production materials, finished goods, engineering spares, and other stock items to prevent financial exposure and ensure accurate financial control of inventory within Flour Operations.


The Job&nbsp;


	Ensure all inventory transactions and stock transfers are correctly posted in the system.
	Perform monthly reconciliation of inventory sub-ledger with the general ledger and resolve all variances.
	Ensure adequate provision is made for stock losses and prevent prior-year adjustments.
	Implement proper controls and approval processes for stock write-offs.
	Monitor stock status to ensure correct item setup and accurate costing at all times.
	Ensure there are no negative stock balances or incorrect inventory postings.
	Monitor transit locations to ensure transfers are completed within standard timelines.
	Reconcile manual inventory records with system records to ensure accuracy.
	Prepare weekly and monthly inventory reports, including movement analysis and stock level reviews.
	Ensure all spare parts transactions are correctly recorded and monitored.


Qualifications


	B.Sc. / HND in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related Social Science discipline.
	Professional qualification (ACA /ACCA or equivalent) is required.
	Master&rsquo;s degree will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 5 years of experience, with at least 3 years in manufacturing finance or inventory control.
	Experience in FMCG or manufacturing environment will be an added advantage.


The Person Must:


	Have good interpersonal and communication skills
	Possess strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Have good knowledge of inventory control and manufacturing finance.
	Be able to work under strict deadlines and reporting timelines.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-25",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-26",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",

       "streetAddress": ". ",

    }

Inventory Control Accountant Job at Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company's flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Control Accountant

Job Location: Apapa, Lagos

Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To effectively monitor all inventory transactions relating to production materials, finished goods, engineering spares, and other stock items to prevent financial exposure and ensure accurate financial control of inventory within Flour Operations.


The Job 


	Ensure all inventory transactions and stock transfers are correctly posted in the system.
	Perform monthly reconciliation of inventory sub-ledger with the general ledger and resolve all variances.
	Ensure adequate provision is made for stock losses and prevent prior-year adjustments.
	Implement proper controls and approval processes for stock write-offs.
	Monitor stock status to ensure correct item setup and accurate costing at all times.
	Ensure there are no negative stock balances or incorrect inventory postings.
	Monitor transit locations to ensure transfers are completed within standard timelines.
	Reconcile manual inventory records with system records to ensure accuracy.
	Prepare weekly and monthly inventory reports, including movement analysis and stock level reviews.
	Ensure all spare parts transactions are correctly recorded and monitored.


Qualifications


	B.Sc. / HND in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related Social Science discipline.
	Professional qualification (ACA /ACCA or equivalent) is required.
	Master’s degree will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 5 years of experience, with at least 3 years in manufacturing finance or inventory control.
	Experience in FMCG or manufacturing environment will be an added advantage.


The Person Must:


	Have good interpersonal and communication skills
	Possess strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Have good knowledge of inventory control and manufacturing finance.
	Be able to work under strict deadlines and reporting timelines.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Quality Control / Quality Assurance Engineer",
  "description": "Fort Hermon Limited is a dynamic engineering and construction company specializing in delivering high-quality infrastructure, building, and industrial projects. With a focus on innovation, safety, and technical excellence, we provide end-to-end solutions that meet global standards and exceed client expectations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control / Quality Assurance Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Gusau, Zamfara
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
Quality Assurance &amp; Compliance:


	Develop and implement Project Quality Plans (PQP), Inspection &amp; Test Plans (ITP), and Method Statements.
	Ensure compliance with project specifications, industry standards, and regulatory requirements.
	Monitor adherence to quality control procedures across all site activities.


Batching Plant &amp; Concrete Works Oversight:


	Supervise and monitor batching plant operations to ensure accurate mix proportions and consistency.
	Review and validate concrete mix designs in line with structural requirements.
	Ensure proper calibration and maintenance of batching plant equipment.
	Oversee production, transportation, and placement of concrete on site.


Material Inspection &amp; Testing:


	Conduct routine inspections and testing of construction materials (cement, aggregates, water, admixtures).
	Ensure proper sampling and laboratory testing of concrete (slump tests, cube tests, compressive strength tests).
	Maintain records of all test results and ensure traceability.


Site Inspection &amp; Quality Control:


	Perform daily site inspections to verify compliance with approved drawings and specifications.
	Identify non-conformances and implement corrective and preventive actions.
	Liaise with site engineers, contractors, and consultants on quality-related matters.


Documentation &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare and maintain quality reports, inspection records, and test documentation.
	Submit daily, weekly, and monthly QA/QC reports to management.
	Maintain proper documentation for audits and handover processes.


Coordination &amp; Training:


	Train site personnel on quality standards, procedures, and best practices.
	Coordinate with consultants and third-party testing agencies where required.
	Support internal and external quality audits.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;vineonyido@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note:&nbsp;Only qualified candidate with hands on experience will be contacted.",

Quality Control / Quality Assurance Engineer Job at Fort Hermon Limited




        
    

        
        Fort Hermon Limited is a dynamic engineering and construction company specializing in delivering high-quality infrastructure, building, and industrial projects. With a focus on innovation, safety, and technical excellence, we provide end-to-end solutions that meet global standards and exceed client expectations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control / Quality Assurance Engineer

Job Location: Gusau, Zamfara
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
Quality Assurance & Compliance:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and implement Project Quality Plans (PQP), Inspection & Test Plans (ITP), and Method Statements.
	Ensure compliance with project specifications, industry standards, and regulatory requirements.
	Monitor adherence to quality control procedures across all site activities.


Batching Plant & Concrete Works Oversight:


	Supervise and monitor batching plant operations to ensure accurate mix proportions and consistency.
	Review and validate concrete mix designs in line with structural requirements.
	Ensure proper calibration and maintenance of batching plant equipment.
	Oversee production, transportation, and placement of concrete on site.


Material Inspection & Testing:


	Conduct routine inspections and testing of construction materials (cement, aggregates, water, admixtures).
	Ensure proper sampling and laboratory testing of concrete (slump tests, cube tests, compressive strength tests).
	Maintain records of all test results and ensure traceability.


Site Inspection & Quality Control:


	Perform daily site inspections to verify compliance with approved drawings and specifications.
	Identify non-conformances and implement corrective and preventive actions.
	Liaise with site engineers, contractors, and consultants on quality-related matters.


Documentation & Reporting:


	Prepare and maintain quality reports, inspection records, and test documentation.
	Submit daily, weekly, and monthly QA/QC reports to management.
	Maintain proper documentation for audits and handover processes.


Coordination & Training:


	Train site personnel on quality standards, procedures, and best practices.
	Coordinate with consultants and third-party testing agencies where required.
	Support internal and external quality audits.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only qualified candidate with hands on experience will be contacted.
